free_text: Double, single differential neutron emission and
total cross sections at 14.1 MeV for B-10, B-11
Preliminary data ADX and SIG given
Correction calculations and MUSCC3 code description
Operated with a pulsed deuteron beam of 2 ns pulse
14 MeV neutrons generated at a Ti-T target
Time-of-flight with flight path of 8.3 m fixed
in the 85 degrees direction. The scattering angles
were changed by moving the sample on an arch, located
17 cm radially apart from the t-target.
A 25.4 cm diameter by 10 cm thick NE-213 liquid
scintillator was used to measure the angular
distributions of scattered neutrons. The detector had
excellent n-gamma discrimination for a dynamic range
of 0.5 to 14 MeV in recoil proton energy. A specially
shaped shadowbar and collimator shield eliminated
based on JENDL-3. In case that JENDL-3 would produce
double differential neutron emission spectrum in
drastic disagreement with the measured one, the
correction can be failed and the adoption of RAW data
.Correction factor are calculated using evaluated data,
however, they are still primitive. Calculation of
correction factors should be carried out again after
new version of evaluation would be published.
.The B-10, B-11 and Bi-209 samples were a cylinders
of 3 cm diameter and 7.0 cm length.
.The monitor sample was a cylinder of polyethylene with
1.5 cm diameter by 5 cm in height.
.Background runs were performed with an empty aluminum
can (B-10, B-11) and with no sample at all.
